problem_33.natural_language: Dini's theorem on an interval: monotone pointwise convergence of continuous functions to a continuous
problem_33.lean:
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Exp
import Mathlib.Topology.MetricSpace.Basic
import Mathlib.Topology.UniformSpace.UniformConvergence
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Topology.Order.IsLUB

open scoped Topology
open Filter
open Set
open Metric
open Uniform
open Uniformity

theorem tendstoUniformlyOn_of_monotone_continuousOn
    (f_n : Nat → Real → Real) (f : Real → Real) (a b : Real)
    (hmono : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, Monotone (fun n => f_n n x))
    (hcont : ∀ n, ContinuousOn (f_n n) (Set.Icc a b))
    (hcont_lim : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc a b))
    (hpt : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, Tendsto (fun n => f_n n x) atTop (nhds (f x))) :
    TendstoUniformlyOn (fun n x => f_n n x) f atTop (Set.Icc a b) := by
  intro u hu
  have h_basis : (𝓤 ℝ).HasBasis (fun ε : ℝ => 0 < ε) fun ε => { p : ℝ × ℝ | dist p.1 p.2 < ε} := by
    exact uniformity_basis_dist
  have h : ∃ ε > 0, { p : ℝ × ℝ | dist p.1 p.2 < ε } ⊆ u := by
    have h_mem : (u ∈ 𝓤 ℝ) := hu
    have h_basis : (𝓤 ℝ).HasBasis (fun ε : ℝ => 0 < ε) fun ε => { p : ℝ × ℝ | dist p.1 p.2 < ε} := h_basis
    have h_exists : ∃ ε > 0, { p : ℝ × ℝ | dist p.1 p.2 < ε } ⊆ u := by
      have h_iff : u ∈ 𝓤 ℝ ↔ ∃ ε, 0 < ε ∧ {p : ℝ × ℝ | dist p.1 p.2 < ε} ⊆ u := by
        rw [h_basis.mem_iff']
      rw [h_iff] at h_mem
      exact h_mem
    exact h_exists
  obtain ⟨ε, hε_pos, hε_ball⟩ := h
  
  have h_compact : IsCompact (Set.Icc a b) := by
    exact ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder.isCompact_Icc a b
  
  have h_unif : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, dist (f x) (f_n n x) < ε := by
    sorry
  
  have h_final : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, ∀ x, x ∈ Set.Icc a b → (f x, f_n n x) ∈ u := by
    sorry
  
  exact h_final
problem_33.natural_language_proof:
limit on `[a,b]` is uniform.

Proof: fix `eps > 0` and let `K = [a,b]`. For each `n`, set
`U_n = {x in K | f x - f_n n x < eps}`. Since `f` and `f_n n` are continuous on `K`, each `U_n` is
open in `K`. Monotonicity in `n` implies `U_n ⊆ U_{n+1}`. Also pointwise convergence implies that
for every `x ∈ K` we have `f_n n x -> f x`, so eventually `f x - f_n n x < eps`, hence
`⋃ n, U_n = K`.
Because `K` is compact, an increasing open cover has a single set that already covers: there is
`N` with `U_N = K`. Thus for all `x ∈ K`, `0 ≤ f x - f_n N x < eps`, which is exactly uniform
convergence on `K`.
